message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I'm implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Let me know your feature/files so we can keep them disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My feature: Sync Features dictionaries - add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to keep _column_requires_decoding in sync with main dict. What is your feature/files?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Our features both touch Features in the same file but different methods (my update() deep-merge vs your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator). Unavoidable overlap - proceeding independently, will rely on git merge at the end since edits are in different locations. I'll keep my changes scoped to update().

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py (unavoidable - same Features class). Proposal to minimize collision: I will only touch __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__ (and add a new top-level keep_features_dicts_synced decorator function near the class), and I will NOT touch the body of update(). Please keep your deep-merge changes scoped to update() only and avoid touching __setitem__/__delitem__ directly. That keeps our edits in distinct line ranges so merge conflicts should be minimal/resolvable. Confirm ACCEPT if OK, or REVISE with alternative.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Confirmed. I'll touch __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__ + new decorator, you own update(). Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (update() method only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (__init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, keep_features_dicts_synced decorator)
note: Confirmed. I will scope my deep-merge changes strictly to the update() method body and not touch __setitem__/__delitem__/__init__. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (update() method + _update_value/_deep_merge_dict helpers)
note: Deep-merge update() implementation complete and tests passing (tests/features/test_features.py). Ready to merge whenever you're done.
